notes: US nonprofit operating federally funded research and development centers (FFRDCs) for the US government. Created and maintains CVE, CWE, ATT&CK, CAPEC, and ATLAS — foundational identifier systems and frameworks for cybersecurity. In April 2025, MITRE's CVE contract nearly expired with short notice; CISA extended funding, prompting formation of the CVE Foundation as a nonprofit to ensure long-term stability.

data.description: The canonical vulnerability identifier system, operated by MITRE under contract with CISA. CVE IDs are the standard cross-reference for vulnerability advisories worldwide.
A taxonomy of software and hardware weakness types. Used by NVD, OWASP, and vulnerability databases to classify root causes of vulnerabilities.
Knowledge base of adversary tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) based on real-world observations. The standard framework for threat modeling and detection engineering.

match_nodes.description: Adversarial Threat Landscape for AI Systems

data.official_name: Adversarial Threat Landscape for AI Systems
data.description: Knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques targeting AI/ML systems. Extends ATT&CK concepts to the AI domain.

match_nodes.description: Common Attack Pattern Enumeration and Classification

data.official_name: Common Attack Pattern Enumeration and Classification
data.description: A taxonomy of attack patterns — how adversaries exploit weaknesses. Complementary to CWE (which describes the weaknesses themselves).
